task_description: Find a top-rated women's fragrance and look up/select its 30 ml product option for price comparison on an online perfume store.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on an empty white area just below the browser's top bar, indicating a click on a blank region rather than on a button, tab, or icon. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to a new tab page with the Google logo, a central search box, shortcut icons below it, and a dropdown suggestion panel hanging from the address bar at the top. The X is positioned in the page background area, away from any visible control.
caption.action: Click the blank white page area under the top bar.
caption.expectation: The page remains on the new tab screen, and the address bar suggestion dropdown should lose focus and close.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows Chrome's address bar active with the text "most popular women fragrances" entered, and a blue text cursor at the end indicating typing into the omnibox/search field rather than a page form. A dropdown of Google search suggestions is visible directly below, including entries such as "most popular women fragrance 2025," "most popular women's fragrance 2024," and "most popular women's fragrance of all time," so the typed text appears to replace the previously empty field and form a search query. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on a new tab page with the Google layout, shortcut icons in the center, and the suggestion panel expanded under the top bar.
caption.action: Type "MOST POPULAR WOMEN FRAGRANCES" into the browser address bar search field.
caption.expectation: The query text should appear in the address bar and the autocomplete suggestions should update to related searches for popular women's fragrances.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a plain light gray and white portion of the autocomplete dropdown area beneath the browser's address bar; no specific button or icon is highlighted as a click target. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on a new tab page with the address bar containing the search text "most popular women fragrances" and a Google suggestions panel expanded below it, listing related searches such as "most popular women's fragrances 2025," "2024," and "of all time."
caption.expectation: Chrome should submit the current query and load a Google search results page for most popular women fragrances.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the Google results page itself with no semitransparent red X, indicating a scroll action affecting the page rather than a specific control. Visible content includes the Google logo and search bar with the query "most popular women fragrances," a results section featuring a paragraph summary with highlighted perfume names such as Chanel Coco Mademoiselle, Valentino Donna Born In Roma, and Prada Paradoxe, an "展开" button below the summary, a right-side panel with video and shopping-style result cards, and a lower organic result titled "Best Fragrance of 2025 - 9th Fragrantica Community Awards." In the full-screen context, Chrome is displaying the Google search results page with the vertical scrollbar positioned near the top, so scrolling down will move further into the results list.
caption.action: Scroll down on the Google results page.
caption.expectation: The page should move downward, revealing more search results and related content below the currently visible fragrance results.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the purple underlined search result title text "Best Fragrance of 2025 - 9th Fragrantica Community Awards," indicating the clickable result link itself. The surrounding text includes the source "Fragrantica," the URL line, and a short snippet about Fragrantica Awards voting. In the full-screen view, this is a Google results page in Chrome for "most popular women fragrances," with the clicked result appearing below an expandable summary section and above a related questions list.
caption.action: Click the purple link titled "Best Fragrance of 2025 - 9th Fragrantica Community Awards."
caption.expectation: The selected Google result should open the Fragrantica awards page, navigating away from the results list or opening the page in the current tab.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the full browser page with no semitransparent red X, indicating a scroll action on the page rather than a specific control. Visible on the page is the Fragrantica site header, the large title "Best Fragrance of 2025 - 9th Fragrantica Community Awards," a pink message stating voting is finished, an "All Award Categories" dropdown field, and the top of two award panels labeled "Best Women's Fragrance 2025" and "Best Men's Fragrance 2025" with perfume bottle images. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to the Fragrantica awards page, with a vertical scrollbar near the top and a small Google Translate popup open near the upper-right, so scrolling down will move further into the awards listings.
caption.action: Scroll down on the Fragrantica awards page.
caption.expectation: The page should move downward to reveal more of the fragrance award sections and additional content below the currently visible top categories.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X placed on the heading text for the right award card, specifically over the label "Best Men's Fragrance 2025." This is an object on the page content rather than a browser toolbar control. In the full-screen view, the Fragrantica awards page is open in Chrome, with two large award panels visible side by side: "Best Women's Fragrance 2025" on the left and "Best Men's Fragrance 2025" on the right, each showing perfume bottle images below the title. A Google Translate popup is open near the upper-right of the browser.
caption.action: Click the "Best Men's Fragrance 2025" award panel title area.
caption.expectation: The men's fragrance category should become selected or open its associated category content, such as navigating to that section or showing the category details/winners.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the full browser page with no semitransparent red X, indicating a scroll action affecting the webpage rather than a specific control. The page is already inside the Fragrantica category view titled "Best Women's Fragrance 2025." Near the top are a pale orange "Back to All Awards Categories" button and an orange "+ Add your suggestion" button. Below them is a grid of perfume cards with large bottle images and vote counters, including "Guidance 46 Amouage (unisex) ~ 2024," "Shalimar L'Essence Guerlain (female) ~ 2025," and "Valaya Exclusif Parfums de Marly (female) ~ 2025." The full-screen view confirms this is the Chrome window on the Fragrantica awards site, with more perfume cards partially visible lower down and the page scrollbar positioned near the upper portion, so scrolling down will continue through the women's fragrance rankings.
caption.action: Scroll down on the Fragrantica category page.
caption.expectation: The page should move downward to reveal more perfume entries in the Best Women's Fragrance 2025 list.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red drag path running horizontally across the perfume name text line "Shalimar L'Essence Guerlain (female) ~ 2025," indicating a text-selection style drag over that label. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to the Fragrantica "Best Women's Fragrance 2025" page with a grid of perfume cards. The top row includes Guidance 46 Amouage on the left, Shalimar L'Essence Guerlain in the center, and Valaya Exclusif Parfums de Marly on the right, each with bottle images and vote counters. An orange "+ Add your suggestion" button floats on the right side.
caption.action: Click-and-hold on the left side of the "Shalimar L'Essence Guerlain (female) ~ 2025" text label, drag along the shown path to the right across the full label, then release.
caption.expectation: The fragrance name text should become selected or highlighted, ready for copying or further text-based actions.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the perfume title text "Shalimar L'Essence Guerlain" highlighted in blue within the center perfume card, with the rest of the label continuing as "(female) ~ 2025" to the right. This is selected page text, not a text entry field, so there is no visible typing area and no new characters are being entered; the hotkey would act on the current selection rather than replace or append text. In the full-screen Chrome window, the Fragrantica page "Best Women's Fragrance 2025" displays a grid of perfume cards including Guidance 46 Amouage on the left, Shalimar L'Essence Guerlain in the center, and Valaya Exclusif Parfums de Marly on the right, with an orange "+ Add your suggestion" button floating on the right side.
caption.action: Press CTRL+C to copy the selected "Shalimar L'Essence Guerlain" text.
caption.expectation: The selected perfume name should be copied to the clipboard while the page remains visually unchanged.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the pale blue active browser tab at the top of Chrome. The tab displays the page title beginning with "Best Fragrance of 2025 - 9th" and has a small red Fragrantica-style favicon on the left and a close "X" on the right. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to the Fragrantica "Best Women's Fragrance 2025" page, where the perfume name "Shalimar L'Essence Guerlain" remains highlighted in blue in the center card. The click target is a top-area UI control, specifically the active tab itself rather than page content.
caption.action: Click the active Chrome tab labeled "Best Fragrance of 2025 - 9th".
caption.expectation: The tab should become focused/selected as the active browser tab, with no major page change beyond maintaining focus on this page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered over the bookmark shortcut labeled "QQ邮箱," which has a multicolored circular icon beneath the tab bar on the new tab page. This is a clickable page object, not a toolbar control. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying a new tab with the Google logo, a central search bar, and a grid of site shortcuts including "QQ邮箱" in the lower-left area of the shortcut grid.
caption.action: Click the "QQ邮箱" shortcut tile on the new tab page.
caption.expectation: The QQ Mail website should begin loading in the current tab.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows Chrome's address bar at the top of a new tab page with the text "perfumehub.pl" entered and the text cursor at the end, indicating typing into the omnibox rather than a page field. A suggestion dropdown is open below it, with the top suggestion reading "PerfumeHub - Porównywarka Cen Perfum - perfumehub.pl" and additional suggestions such as a Google search for "perfumehub.pk" and previous perfumehub-related entries. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on a new tab page with the Google logo area, a central search box in Chinese, and shortcut tiles including QQ邮箱 and PerfumeHub; the typed text appears to replace the previously empty address bar content.
caption.action: Type "PERFUMEHUB.PL" into the Chrome address bar.
caption.expectation: The address bar should contain the full website address and Chrome should continue showing matching suggestions for that site.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the top portion of Chrome's address bar suggestion dropdown beneath the new tab tab label, with the typed site name visible and the top autocomplete result reading "PerfumeHub - Porównywarka Cen Perfum - perfumehub.pl". Although a faint red X appears over the dropdown area, the raw action is a keyboard action. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on a new tab page with "perfumehub.pl" entered in the address bar, a list of matching suggestions expanded below, and the Google new tab shortcuts visible underneath.
caption.action: Press ENTER to submit the address bar entry "perfumehub.pl".
caption.expectation: Chrome should accept the address bar entry, close the suggestions dropdown, and begin loading the PerfumeHub website in the current tab.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the main search field near the top of the PerfumeHub page. The field contains the placeholder text beginning with "Wpisz nazwę perfum ...", indicating a text box for entering a perfume name. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on the PerfumeHub homepage with the PerfumeHub logo at upper left, product listings in the center, category and filter links on the left, a red search button with a magnifying glass to the right of the search field, a Google Translate popup near the top right, and a cookie consent banner along the bottom. The target is a UI control in the page content area.
caption.action: Click the perfume-name search text box labeled with the placeholder "Wpisz nazwę perfum ...".
caption.expectation: The search field should become active with a text cursor inside it, ready for typing a perfume name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the active search text box near the top center of the PerfumeHub page, outlined in blue, with the placeholder text "Wpisz nazwę perfum ..." still visible, indicating no pasted text is visible yet in the field. The full-screen view shows the PerfumeHub homepage in Chrome, with the PerfumeHub logo at upper left, a red search button with a white magnifying-glass icon attached to the right of the search field, product listings in the middle, category and filter sections on the left, and a cookie consent banner across the bottom.
caption.action: Press CTRL+V to paste the copied perfume name into the active search field, replacing the placeholder text.
caption.expectation: The search box should display the pasted perfume name and remain active, ready for submitting the search.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the single autocomplete suggestion directly below the search box. The highlighted suggestion text reads "Guerlain Shalimar L'essence" and appears as a light gray dropdown row under the typed query. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to the PerfumeHub website. The search field at the top center contains "Shalimar L'Essence Guerlain", a red search button with a white magnifying-glass icon is attached on the right, the suggestion dropdown is open under the field, product listings are visible below, category and filter panels are on the left, and a cookie consent banner spans the bottom. The target is a UI control in the page content area.
caption.action: Click the autocomplete suggestion "Guerlain Shalimar L'essence" below the search box.
caption.expectation: The suggestion should be selected, likely loading or filtering to the matching perfume result page or search results for Guerlain Shalimar L'essence.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the rectangular size option labeled "30 ml" with the price line "od 339.00 zł" beneath it. This is a selectable product variant tile in the page content area, not a toolbar control. In the full-screen view, the PerfumeHub product page for "Guerlain Shalimar L'essence" is open, with the perfume bottle image on the left, the product title and concentration tabs near the center, several size/variant boxes in a row, seller listings below, and a cookie consent banner at the bottom.
caption.action: Click the "30 ml" variant tile showing "od 339.00 zł".
caption.expectation: The 30 ml option should become selected, and the page should update the offers or product details to reflect the chosen size.
